Title: A balance between glycitein and glyceollins governed by isoflavone 6-hydroxylase confers soybean resistance to Phytophthora sojae

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Daidzein-to-glycitein branch; 6-hydroxylation branch of isoflavone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Mid-to-late tailoring step; branch point competing with daidzein 2'-hydroxylation/glyceollin branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes A-ring C6 hydroxylation of daidzein to form 6-hydroxydaidzein, the direct precursor of glycitein; also hydroxylates formononetin and biochanin A in yeast assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product and diverts flux toward glycitein-type isoflavonoids away from glyceollin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for glycitein-type isoflavonoid accumulation; precise dosage-dependent control of defense flux supported, strict rate-limiting status not explicitly established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predominantly governs glycitein-type isoflavonoid biosynthesis in soybean; loss of function nearly abolishes glycitein-type isoflavonoids, whereas overexpression elevates 6-hydroxydaidzein derivatives and alters defense metabolite partitioning, reducing glyceollin I during infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts in soybean defense as phytoanticipin-related metabolite class and contributes to resistance against Phytophthora sojae; isoflavonoids also have antioxidant and human health-promoting properties
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mature roots; hypocotyls of 2-d-old germinating soybean seedlings; seeds for natural variation profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Preferentially expressed in mature roots and hypocotyls of soybean seedlings; expression unchanged upon Phytophthora sojae infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Origin_Species: Preferentially expressed in mature roots and hypocotyls
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phytophthora sojae infection; daidzein-d6 feeding; germinating hypocotyls; yeast substrate feeding; ion/pH/temperature assays
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive overexpression in transgenic soybean)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolome GWAS using LC-qToF-MS isoflavonoid profiling of 727 soybean accessions; transcriptome profiling of inoculated hypocotyls; resequencing/SNP data from prior study including 53 soja accessions; database accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both loss and overexpression increase susceptibility to Phytophthora sojae; overexpression reroutes flux away from glyceollin I accumulation; domestication-associated low-activity haplotype reduces glycitein-type isoflavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Formerly referred to as GmCYP76F17 and redesignated GmIF6H1. In vitro activity is higher toward formononetin and biochanin A than daidzein, but in planta evidence establishes daidzein as the bona fide substrate. A T248A polymorphism underlies haplotype differences in catalytic efficiency and glycitein content. Trace glycitein in mutants after infection suggests possible minor alternative route(s). Possible metabolon with IFS and 2HID is proposed but not demonstrated
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; soybean hairy root overexpression; yeast substrate-feeding assay; microsomal enzyme assay and kinetics; subcellular localization; CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; stable-isotope tracing with daidzein-d6; LC-MS metabolite profiling; pathogen assay; chemical complementation with glycitein
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering glycitein content and disease-resilient soybean, but expression must be finely tuned because both knockout and strong overexpression reduce resistance; haplotype selection/editing at residue 248 may optimize glycitein traits; should be considered together with glyceollin pathway genes in stacking strategies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmIF6H1/Glyma.11g108300 was identified by mGWAS as the major gene underlying glycitein-type isoflavonoid variation in soybean. 2. Biochemical assays and stable-isotope tracing showed that it hydroxylates daidzein at C6 to produce 6-hydroxydaidzein, establishing the predominant biosynthetic route to glycitein in planta. 3. CRISPR loss-of-function mutants nearly lost glycitein-type isoflavonoids and accumulated more daidzin/malonyldaidzin. 4. Overexpression increased 6-hydroxydaidzein derivatives and changed defense metabolite partitioning. 5. Both deficiency and overexpression increased susceptibility to Phytophthora sojae, demonstrating that balanced flux between glycitein-type isoflavonoids and glyceollins is essential. 6. A domestication-associated T248A substitution reduces enzyme efficiency and glycitein accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates daidzein at the 2' position to initiate glyceollin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor branch for glyceollin phytoalexin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the competing branch enzyme directing daidzein toward glyceollin biosynthesis; reduced glyceollin I accumulation in GmIF6H1 overexpression lines is discussed in relation to this branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Inducible phytoalexins that inhibit Phytophthora sojae and contribute to pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inoculated hypocotyls during Phytophthora sojae infection
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Infection-inducible pathway context discussed; specific expression pattern not resolved here
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome profiling of inoculated hypocotyls referenced for isoflavone-pathway genes; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition for daidzein pool with GmIF6H1 may affect defense balance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a directly relevant study-species pathway gene in the defense model and Discussion, but not functionally manipulated in this paper; precise mechanism of perturbation by GmIF6H1 overexpression remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Metabolite profiling during infection; inferential pathway model from this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene-stacking target with GmIF6H1 to optimize balance between phytoanticipin and phytoalexin defenses, but direct engineering recommendations are inferential here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmI2'H is presented as the daidzein 2'-hydroxylase branch enzyme leading to glyceollin biosynthesis. 2. The study proposes that daidzein allocation between GmIF6H1 and GmI2'H underlies balanced defense against Phytophthora sojae. 3. Reduced glyceollin I in GmIF6H1 overexpression plants suggests flux competition with the GmI2'H branch, although GmI2'H itself was not directly tested here.
